info.title: Vast.ai API
info.description: API for managing GPU cloud resources on Vast.ai

post.description: Create multiple instances in one request.  Request body accepts either: - ids: [<ask_id>, ...] - ask_ids: [<ask_id>, ...] Other params (image, env, disk, etc.) are applied uniformly to each ask, mirroring asks_accept behavior.

put.description: Creates a new instance contract set when a client accepts the 'ask' contract set from a provider.  Security: Authentication enforced via security.get_request_info_user_base(abort=True) - Guarantees `user` object exists and is valid - Guarantees `user_id` is a valid integer matching authenticated user - Template ownership validation uses user_id for access control

post.description: Sets the user's Bitbucket API token in user_secrets

get.description: Allows the client to fetch their own bitbucket auth info. This will also refresh a user's expired Bitbucket OAuth token if expired

put.description: Refreshes the Bitbucket repo and PR data

get.description: #return { "offers" : []} query_str = None query = None try: query_str = request.GET.get("q", "{}") query = json.loads(query_str) except Exception as e: return { "error": "invalid" }  return bundles_search_endpoint_(request, query_str, query)

post.description: query = None try: query = request.json_body except Exception as e: return { "error": "invalid" }  query_str = json.dumps(query) return bundles_search_endpoint_(request, query_str, query)

post.summary: Create daemon report self test results

get.summary: Get instance price increase accept token

post.summary: Create instance price increase accept token

get.description: Gets a mapping of unique filterable values for all of the user's instances

post.description: Sets the user's JIRA API token in user_secrets

get.description: Allows the client to fetch their own JIRA auth info. This will also refresh a user's expired JIRA OAuth token if expired

put.description: Takes in instance type and launch info, searches offers accordingly and takes the first result and creates an instance with it

get.description: Get a single legion agent by ID.

post.description: Create a new legion agent.

get.description: List all or specified legion agents for user.
List all repositories for the authenticated user.
Get a single legion task by ID.

post.description: Create a new legion task.

put.description: Update an existing legion task. - ADMIN ONLY

get.description: List all legion tasks.

post.description: Register a new machine with full hardware info (GPUs, CPUs, disks, network).  Column allowlists prevent injection of verification, hosting_type, etc. In dev mode the allowlist is relaxed so the market sim can create machines that appear in search.

get.description: Return active maintenance windows for host-owned machines. Intended for CLI use.

post.description: Schedule maintenance windows for multiple machines.  Body params: machine_ids          list[int]  — machines to schedule maintenance for sdate                float      — start time (unix timestamp, must be in the future) duration             int|float  — duration in hours maintenance_category str        — one of: power, internet, disk, gpu, software, other

delete.description: Delete multiple maintenance windows by ID.  Body params: maintenance_ids  list[int]  — maintenance record IDs to delete

post.description: Transfers ownership of machines from the current owner to another user.  This endpoint allows a host to transfer ownership of their machines to another user. It updates the owner_id in the machines table and the host_id in any associated non-deleted contracts and active ask contract offers.
deletes machine if the machine is not being used by clients. host jobs on their own machines are disregarded and machine is force deleted.

get.description: Returns current snapshot of all GPU types, not time-series data.
Returns time-series supply/demand, pricing, and stats per GPU type.

Returns GPU machine locations for a geo map dashboard. Coordinates are rounded to 4 decimal places to merge nearby IPs into a single data point. No need to store in VictoriaMetrics since only current locations matter, not historical.

put.description: Accept a network volume ask and initiate volume creation.  This view handles a PUT request to accept a network volume ask, validating the request data, performing rate limiting and user authentication, and triggering the creation of a network volume. It logs audit records and handles invalid input with appropriate HTTP responses.  Args: request (Request): A Pyramid request with additional callables to execute SQLAlchemy queries.

get.description: Handles Bitbucket redirect: exchanges code→tokens and stores them
Connects a Vast user to their Discord account using OAuth2
Handles JIRA redirect: exchanges code→tokens and stores them
Returns a list of OAuth service names for the true user from the user_oauth_services table

delete.description: Deletes a user's OAuth service integration

get.description: Generates Discord OAuth URL with a one-time state for CSRF protection

Disconnect a user from Stripe Connect (Express, hosted onboarding flow).  - Does NOT call OAuth deauthorize (not used for Express hosted onboarding). - Clears our linkage and deprecated token fields: connect_user_id = NULL connect_publishable_key = NULL connect_access_token   = NULL connect_refresh_token  = NULL payout_enabled         = false - Returns JSON; never hard-fails the user due to Stripe transient issues.
JSON variant: returns a one-time Stripe Express dashboard login link. Success: { "path": "<https://dashboard.stripe.com/...>" }

Start hosted onboarding (Accounts API + Account Link).  - Create Express connected account on first call - Always create a single-use Account Link and return its URL  Success (200): { "path": "<https://connect.stripe.com/...>" }

put.description: Regenerate API keys for one or all subaccounts owned by the caller.

get.description: Get All Active API Keys for a Team Owner/Manager and who they belong to
Get All Active Member Sessions for a Team Owner/Manager

Run OpenSearch with custom params. It'll return template ids. Pull the templates with those ids from the db  OpenSearch looks for query string matches inside these columns: ["name", "hash_id", "image", "tag", "desc", "command", "repo", "args_str"]  e.g. #1: {"query": "mars"} -> Returns any template that has a fuzzy value for "mars" in above columns e.g. #2: {"query": "mars", "select_filters":{"hash_id":{"eq":"49c538d097ad6437413b83711c9f61e8"}}} -> Returns Everything from Example 1 but filtered so that only results with specified hash_id are returned e.g. #3: { "query": "mars", "order_by": [{"col": "created_at", "dir": "desc"}]} -> Returns everything from Example 1 but ordered by the created_at column and in descending order.

put.description: Takes a template JSON and attempts to update it in both DB and OpenSearch Index

post.description: Log a user in by verifying a given 2FA code and tfa_method  Requirements: - tfa_method - the 2FA method being used ("sms" or "totp" or "email") - code - the 2FA code provided by the user to verify - tfa_secret - the secret token provided to the user in the previous step of auth either from the UI or CLI (if using SMS) - (optional) backup_code - a one time use backup code that can be used if the user is unable to access their normal 2FA method  UI/Client: Called by a user using a valid tfa_secret that was created after passing the first step of auth (username/password)  CLI flow: The user calls this endpoint using their API Key as a Bearer token and a tfa_secret only if using SMS 2FA  On Success: A new 2FA Session API Key that expires in 24 hours will be created for the user (and returned in the response if called from CLI) An audit log record will be created for the successful 2FA login

delete.description: Remove (disable) a specific 2FA method after verifying the request.  Requires one of: - SMS code (tfa_method="sms", code=xxxxxx) - Authenticator TOTP code (tfa_method="totp", code=xxxxxx) - Email code (tfa_method="email", code=xxxxxx) - Backup code (backup_code=XXXX-XXXX-XXXX)  Also requires specifying which method to remove via: - tfa_method_id (preferred), OR - tfa_method ("sms" or "totp" or "email")  On success: - soft-delete the specified user_tfa_methods row (deleted_at) - if no active methods remain: set users.tfa_enabled=False and soft-delete all tfa_backup_codes. - audit log + email notification ("2FA method removed.")

post.description: Authorize adding a new 2FA method by verifying the user's identity with an existing 2FA method or backup code.  Params: - tfa_method: "email" | "sms" | "totp" (auto-forced to "email" if user has no active methods) - tfa_method_id: ID of existing method to verify with (required for sms/totp) - backup_code: use a backup code to authorize directly (skips the send/verify flow)  Returns {"success": True, "secret": tfa_secret} on success (tfa_secret needed for PUT step). Returns {"success": True, "msg": "Authorization successful."} when backup_code is used.

put.description: Authorize adding a new 2FA method - Step 2: Verify the code sent in Step 1.  Params: - code: the 2FA code received (email/SMS code or TOTP code from authenticator app) - secret: the tfa_secret returned from the POST step

post.description: Confirm a new 2FA method by verifying a code.  - SMS flow: verifies Twilio code using two_factor_challenge + phone_number. After success, inserts user_tfa_methods row with method="sms" if not present. - Authenticator flow: verifies TOTP code against provided secret If valid, encrypts secret and inserts user_tfa_methods row with method="totp". - Generates and returns backup codes if this is the user's first ever 2FA method. - Sends email notification when 2FA method is added.
Send a 2FA code to the user's email address

put.description: Regenerate backup codes.  Requires a validated 2FA action: - SMS: provide {"tfa_method":"sms","code":...,"secret":...} (secret from api.tfa.sms) - Authenticator: provide {"tfa_method":"totp","code":...} - Email: provide {"tfa_method":"email","code":...,"secret":...} (secret from api.tfa.email)  On success: - soft-deletes any existing backup codes - generates and returns 10 new backup codes (plaintext returned once) - sends an email notification

post.description: Set up SMS 2FA by sending the 2FA code to the user's phone number via Twilio

get.description: Get the current 2FA status for the user, including whether 2FA is enabled, what methods are configured, and how many backup codes they have remaining.

post.description: Confirm a new 2FA method by verifying a code.  - SMS flow: verifies Twilio code using two_factor_challenge + phone_number. After success, inserts user_tfa_methods row with method="sms" if not present. - Authenticator flow: verifies TOTP code against provided secret If valid, encrypts secret and inserts user_tfa_methods row with method="totp". - Generates and returns backup codes if this is the user's first ever 2FA method. - Sends email notification when 2FA method is added.
Set up SMS 2FA by sending the 2FA code to the user's phone number via Twilio
Generate a TOTP secret + provisioning URI for Authenticator-app 2FA setup. Client should display secret/QR and then confirm by calling api.tfa.confirm_new

put.description: Update a 2FA method's label or primary status.  Requires specifying which method to update via: - tfa_method_id (preferred), OR - tfa_method ("sms" or "totp" but not "email", since email is not stored in user_tfa_methods)  Can update: - label: A user-friendly label for the method (e.g., "Work Phone", "Personal Authenticator") - is_primary: Mark this as the primary/preferred method (only one can be primary)  On success: - Updates the specified user_tfa_methods row - If setting is_primary=True, unsets it for all other methods - Returns updated method info

get.description: Get user total earnings data
Get user earnings data (referral & machine), given a date range.

This endpoint returns the user information related to the owner of the provided API key It ignores the user override effect that happens in user_current_get and is used by the Frontend to know who is currently logged in, even when the user is overridden

post.description: Add credits using a saved Stripe payment method.

creates a bitpay invoice (allows user to pay for this invoice using bitpay) and updates the invoices table. bitpay create invoice documentation for reference (https://developer.bitpay.com/reference/create-an-invoice)
Creates a Crypto.com Pay payment session and stores a pending invoice for webhook fulfillment.

get.description: List the authenticated user's webhook URLs.

post.description: Register a new webhook URL for the authenticated user.

put.description: Update an existing webhook URL for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Delete a webhook URL for the authenticated user.

get.description: Fetches the Audit Logs of a user or team using Standard Query This function allows for any combination of columns to be selected, ordered, and paginated. The function also lets you choose your desired filters and limit. Lastly, it also has the option to add emails to the results, which is useful for Team Owners
List instances for the user using standard query and keyset pagination
List instances for the user using keyset pagination with only columns needed for instances UI

Run OpenSearch with custom params. It'll return template ids. Pull the templates with those ids from the db  OpenSearch looks for query string matches inside these columns: ["name", "hash_id", "image", "tag", "desc", "command", "repo", "args_str"]  e.g. #1: {"query": "mars"} -> Returns any template that has a fuzzy value for "mars" in above columns e.g. #2: {"query": "mars", "select_filters":{"hash_id":{"eq":"49c538d097ad6437413b83711c9f61e8"}}} -> Returns Everything from Example 1 but filtered so that only results with specified hash_id are returned e.g. #3: { "query": "mars", "order_by": [{"col": "created_at", "dir": "desc"}]} -> Returns everything from Example 1 but ordered by the created_at column and in descending order.

post.description: Fetch tags for a specific image, optionally with credentials for private repos.  Routing: - Public Docker Hub  → get_docker_tags_v3 (Redis-cached, stale fallback) - Private Docker Hub → _fetch_dockerhub_tags (direct API, with credentials) - NVIDIA NGC         → get_ngc_tags (Redis-cached) - GHCR/GCR/custom    → _fetch_registry_tags (OCI Distribution API)
Validate Docker registry credentials and list accessible repositories.
